Calculation.ActorValueAdditionHelp: Addition supplied by the range containing the character's current configured value.
Calculation.ActorValueMultiplierHelp: Multiplier supplied by the range containing the character's current configured value.
Calculation.LevelAdditionHelp: Character level multiplied by its configured source multiplier.
Calculation.ProficiencyAdditionHelp: The character's proficiency bonus multiplied by its configured source multiplier.
Calculation.CheckProficiencyAdditionHelp: The proficiency or expertise contribution of the selected check multiplied by its configured source multiplier.
Calculation.AdditionalHelp: Combined additive modifiers configured for this station.
Calculation.BaseProgressHelp: Progress supplied by the station for every unit of downtime.
Calculation.DowntimeHelp: Downtime invested in the completed roll interval.
Calculation.OtherMultipliersHelp: Combined multiplicative modifiers configured for this station.
Calculation.RollAdditionHelp: Progress added by the matching result-table row.
Calculation.RollMultiplierHelp: Progress multiplier from the matching result-table row.
Calculation.StationModifierHelp: Fixed progress addition configured in General.

Dashboard.Title: Downtime overview
Dashboard.Empty: No project progress has been recorded yet.
Dashboard.RemoveProjectConfirm: Cancel {project} for {actor} and permanently remove its saved progress? Spent resources and downtime are not refunded.
Dashboard.ProjectRemoved: The project was cancelled and removed.

Project.AllowedChecksHint: Optional restriction. A project can only restrict checks allowed by the station.
Project.CategoriesHint: Select one or more categories configured in Module Settings. Personal projects without a matching category are hidden; public projects are always allowed.
Project.CharacterRewardsHint: Languages and proficiencies are granted directly to the character. Existing equal or higher proficiencies are kept unchanged.
Project.TalentRewardHint: Talents and other document-based features are granted by dropping their Item into this reward list.
Project.CancelSharedConfirm: Cancel this collaborative project completely? Spent downtime and resources are not refunded.
Project.Name: Name
Project.RollTableHint: Optional. When configured, this table takes priority over the station's roll result table.
Project.RewardRollRange: {minimum}–{maximum} per unit, depending on the roll result
Project.RewardStationDetermined: Determined by the station roll result table

Crafting.Name: Crafting project
Crafting.Description: Create an item in a collaborative project. Use its list price as progress, 50% as material cost, and normally no completion check.

Research.Name: Research project
Research.Description: Collaboratively research knowledge, recipes, or clues. The GM defines progress, costs, reward, and any optional completion check.

Work.Name: Earn a living
Work.Description: Work for 5 downtime, make a skill or tool check, and earn the configured wage scaled by this workplace's reputation.

Training.Name: Training: Language
Training.Description: Language baseline with 600 progress. Adjust target, weekly cost, and reward for weapons (1,200), skill/tool (1,800), armor (2,400), or talent (3,600).

Carousing.Name: Carousing
Carousing.Description: Spend 5 downtime in a social class. Configure 10, 50, or 250 gp weekly cost; the GM determines contacts and favors.

Recovery.Name: Recovery
Recovery.Description: Spend downtime on repeatable recovery or treatment. Configure the desired effect as a reward Item.

RollTable.Label: Result label
RollTable.PresetHint: Choose a quick setup preset to replace the current result table.

Crafting.Name: Progress table
Crafting.Description: A result table with success bands that modify project progress.

Standard.Name: Reputation table
Standard.Description: A result table with reward scaling and reputation-value changes.

Station.CategoriesHint: Add any number of categories. Selected categories appear beside the chooser and can be removed individually.
Station.DropPublicProject: Drop normal items or downtime projects here
Station.DropRequiredTool: Drop an optional station tool here
Station.NoProjects: No projects are available at this station.
Station.RemoveConfirm: Remove downtime-station functionality from {name}? Existing character progress is retained.
Station.RemoveProjectConfirm: Remove {project} from this station? The underlying Item and existing character progress are retained.
Station.FormulaExampleActor: Example values from {actor}; the selected check uses the first allowed check.
Station.FormulaExampleNone: No active character selected; character values are shown as zero.
Station.RollTableHint: Natural 1 and 20 use their fixed rows when enabled. Empty maximum values have no upper bound.
Station.SelectCrafter: Select a character token or assign a user character.

SessionHistory.Name: Enable session history
SessionHistory.Hint: Creates journal entries for session rewards and monthly settlements. When disabled, sessions require no title or description.

StationCategories.Name: Station categories
StationCategories.Hint: Maintain the categories available to stations and projects. IDs are stable matching keys; labels are shown in the interface.

PlayerActorFolders.Name: Actor folders for player selection
PlayerActorFolders.Title: Player selection by Actor folder
PlayerActorFolders.SettingHint: Limit PCs in session management and direct downtime to one root Actor folder and its subfolders.
PlayerActorFolders.Hint: With no selection, all PCs are shown. Select one root folder by its full path; all subfolders are automatically included in session management and direct downtime.
PlayerActorFolders.Invalid: Select an Actor folder from the suggestion list or clear the field.

Tooltips.AddDefaultCostItem: Add the required item configured in the module settings.

Actions.AddModifier: Add another fixed additive or multiplicative modifier.
Actions.AddRollRow: Add another configurable result range to this station.
Actions.AddTier: Add another station-value range and its progress modifiers.
Actions.CreateProject: Create a new project Item from the configured base Item.
Actions.RemoveEntry: Remove this Item from the list. The source Item is not deleted.
Actions.RemoveProject: Remove this public project from the station without deleting its Item or character progress.
Actions.ResetRollTable: Reload the currently selected table preset and discard changes to its rows.
Actions.LoadValueTierPreset: Load the predefined integer reward-multiplier ranges.
Actions.ToggleAbilities: Select all ability checks, or clear them when all are already selected.
Actions.ToggleAllChecks: Select every check, or clear all checks when every check is already selected.
Actions.ToggleSkills: Select all skill checks, or clear them when all are already selected.
Actions.ToggleTools: Select all Black Flag tool checks, or clear them when all are already selected.

Station.General: Basic station identity and the values used by every project at this station.
Station.Enabled: Disabled stations remain configured but cannot start or advance projects.
Station.DisplayName: Optional name shown instead of the station Actor name.
Station.Categories: Categories offered by this station. Personal inventory projects require at least one matching category.
Station.Description: Text shown to characters when they open this station.
Station.BaseProgress: Starting additive progress value for each downtime unit before table results and multipliers.
Station.Modifier: A fixed additive modifier applied by this station to every progress calculation.
Station.RollInterval: Downtime required before a check becomes mandatory. No further downtime can be invested until that check is resolved.
Station.EvaluationMode: Choose whether normal result ranges use the natural d20 face or the complete check total including bonuses.
Station.AllowedChecks: Checks available at this station. Projects may restrict this selection but cannot add other checks.
Station.RequiredTool: Optional tool every character must own and be proficient with to use projects at this station.
Station.Modifiers: Additional fixed additions and multipliers applied after the base and station values.
Station.ModifierOperation: Addition contributes to the value inside parentheses. Multiplication is applied afterward.
Station.ModifierValue: The numeric value of this modifier. Zero and negative multipliers are supported.
Station.RollTable: Maps a roll to an addition and multiplier. Natural-result rows take priority over numeric ranges.
Station.ActorValue: A numeric character value stored either for this station or shared by a selected station category.
Station.PublicProjects: Projects available to every character using this station. Personal projects come from the character's own Items.

RollTable.Enabled: Enables or disables this fixed natural-result row without deleting its settings.
RollTable.Minimum: Lowest result included in this row.
RollTable.Maximum: Highest included result. Leave empty for no upper limit.
RollTable.Label: Name shown to the user when this result occurs.
RollTable.Addition: Added inside the progress formula before multiplication. Negative values can reduce progress.
RollTable.ActorValueChange: Changes the configured character value when this result occurs. It is ignored when that value is disabled.
RollTable.Multiplier: Multiplies the complete additive subtotal. Values below one, zero, and negative values are supported.
RollTable.RewardAddition: Added to every configured reward quantity before multiplication and project batch size.
RollTable.RewardMultiplier: Multiplies every reward quantity after reward addition. Final quantities cannot fall below zero.
RollTable.Natural1: Fixed result row used whenever the natural d20 is 1 and this row is enabled.
RollTable.Natural20: Fixed result row used whenever the natural d20 is 20 and this row is enabled.
RollTable.Preset: Selects a predefined result table. The description of each preset is shown in the option tooltip.

ActorValue.Enabled: Enable tracking and modifiers for this character value. It can belong to this station or be shared by category.
ActorValue.Key: Stable technical key used for storage. Avoid changing it after characters have used the station.
ActorValue.Label: User-facing name of the value, for example Reputation.
ActorValue.Default: Initial value for a character who has never used this station.
ActorValue.Minimum: Optional lower limit applied whenever the value changes.
ActorValue.Maximum: Optional upper limit applied whenever the value changes.
ActorValue.CompletionChange: Station-wide change applied in addition to the project's own completion change.
ActorValue.Tiers: Value ranges can modify progress and reward quantities independently. Their upper bounds are generated automatically from the next higher integer minimum; only the highest range extends to infinity.

Project.General: Identity, progress target, and repeatability of this project.
Project.Description: Explanation shown in the station's project list.
Project.RequiredProgress: Progress required for completion. Progress is stored separately for each character and station.
Project.Repeatable: After completion, grant rewards and reset progress so the same character can perform the project again.
Project.AllowedChecks: Optional subset of the station checks. Selecting checks not enabled at the station has no effect.
Project.Categories: Selected categories determine which stations can offer this project from a character's inventory. Public assignment to a station always overrides this filter.
Project.RequiredTools: Every listed tool must be owned and proficient before the project can start.
Project.RequiredItems: Required materials consumed once when the project starts.
Project.CompletionCosts: Items consumed immediately before rewards are granted on completion.
Project.Rewards: All listed Items are granted automatically on completion. At least one reward is required.

Items.Drop: Drag an Item here to add it to this list.
Items.Quantity: Quantity consumed or granted when this list is processed. Decimal quantities are supported.

DowntimeItem.Title: Downtime item
DowntimeItem.Hint: Configure how this Item grants the owning character downtime.
DowntimeItem.IsProject: This Item is configured as a downtime project.
DowntimeItem.AmountHint: Set this to 0 to display the character's current downtime when the Item is used.
DowntimeItem.Consume: Consume one Item on fallback redemption
DowntimeItem.NativeUse: Use this Item through its native system action. The system action handles consumption; the module then grants the configured downtime.
DowntimeItem.RemoveConfirm: Remove the downtime-item configuration from this Item?
DowntimeItem.MakeProjectHint: Use this Item as a station project or recipe.
DowntimeItem.MakeItemHint: The owning character can redeem this Item for downtime.
DowntimeItem.Description: This Item can be consumed to gain {amount} downtime.
DowntimeItem.DescriptionCurrent: Using this Item displays the character's current downtime.

Errors.EmbeddedRequired: This downtime Item must belong to a character before it can be redeemed.

Errors.Amount: Enter a downtime amount of 0 or greater.
Errors.Busy: This Item is already being redeemed.

Session.AlreadyAwarded: This session has already been awarded.

Errors.Required: Select at least one character. A title is also required when session history is enabled.
Errors.SaveFirst: Enter a title, select at least one character, and save the session first.

Errors.StartNew: Start a new session before changing these values.

StationPresets.Title: Station Presets
StationPresets.Hint: Create editable world Actors from built-in presets. Optional project drafts still need their required items and rewards configured.

smithy.Name: Smithy
smithy.Description: A forge for smithing weapons, armor, and metal goods.

alchemy-lab.Name: Alchemy Laboratory
alchemy-lab.Description: A laboratory for alchemical research and production.

kitchen.Name: Kitchen
kitchen.Description: A workplace for cooking and provisioning.

workplace.Name: Workplace
workplace.Description: Weekly work in 5-downtime blocks with a skill or tool check, wages, and workplace-specific reputation.

library.Name: Research Station
library.Description: Long-term collaborative research with 5 × proficiency progress per downtime and a check every 5 downtime.

training-grounds.Name: Training Station
training-grounds.Description: Training with 10 plus relevant ability and instructor bonus per downtime, a check every 5 downtime, and a final check.

tavern.Name: Carousing Station
tavern.Description: A 5-downtime activity for contacts and favors; social class, costs, and results are configured per project.

workshop.Name: Tinker's Workshop
workshop.Description: A workshop for mechanisms, repairs, and inventions.

tailor.Name: Tailor
tailor.Description: A workshop for tailoring clothing and textile goods.

herbalist.Name: Herbalist
herbalist.Description: A station for herbs, remedies, and recovery preparations.

music-hall.Name: Music Hall
music-hall.Description: A venue for musical practice and performances.

thieves-guild.Name: Thieves' Guild
thieves-guild.Description: A hidden station for thievery and illicit training.

expedition-office.Name: Expedition Office
expedition-office.Description: A planning station for navigation and expeditions.

ProjectLibrary.Title: Project library
ProjectLibrary.Hint: Create and manage world-level downtime projects independently of a station.
ProjectLibrary.Empty: No world-level downtime projects have been created yet.
ProjectLibrary.DeleteConfirm: Permanently delete {project}? It will be removed from {stations} stations and saved progress for affected characters ({actors}) will be deleted. Spent resources are not refunded.
ProjectLibrary.Deleted: The project and its stored assignments were deleted.
ProjectLibrary.SettingHint: Creates, edits, and deletes downtime projects independently of a station.

Help.Title: Downtime Manager – Introduction and Help
Help.Heading: Introduction and help
Help.Introduction: This page explains the complete workflows for Game Masters and players. Each section can also be used independently as a reference.
Help.SettingHint: Explains setup, stations, projects, rewards, sessions, and downtime distribution.

QuickStart.Title: Quick start
QuickStart.Text: The shortest path from an empty world to a usable downtime station with a project.
QuickStart.Point1: Configure a project base Item in Downtime Manager Configuration. This ordinary Item is copied for every new project and supplies its Item type and system data; downtime rules are then added in the project editor.
QuickStart.Point2: Import a station preset or turn an Actor into a downtime station through its header. Configure its name, categories, progress, and checks.
QuickStart.Point3: Then create a blank project or project template directly under the station's Public Projects. A project created there is assigned to the station automatically when saved.
QuickStart.Point4: Configure its target, prerequisites, and costs, and add at least one Item or character reward. The optional default cost Item provides quick access to a frequently used cost.
QuickStart.Point5: Grant downtime through the Downtime Overview or session rewards.
QuickStart.Point6: Players open the station through its token or Actor, start a project, and invest their downtime. To open the station as a user, a GM selects that user's character token, holds Shift, and then opens the station.

Downtime.Title: Downtime balance
Downtime.Text: Downtime is a numeric value on each character and the module's central resource.
Downtime.Point1: Every character has an individual balance stored directly in an Actor flag.
Downtime.Point2: Investments and paid completion-check retries deduct downtime immediately.
Downtime.Point3: Downtime can come from direct grants, sessions, passive grants, or redeemable downtime Items.
Downtime.Point4: Cancelling a project does not automatically refund spent downtime or materials.

Stations.Title: Downtime stations
Stations.Text: Stations determine how characters create progress and which projects are available.
Stations.Point1: A GM can import a built-in station preset or turn any Actor into a station using the hammer control in its header.
Stations.Point2: Enabled controls current availability; display name and description are shown to users.
Stations.Point3: Base progress, progress sources, and additional modifiers form the progress formula.
Stations.Point4: Required checks, roll interval, allowed checks, and result table generally apply to every station project.
Stations.Point5: An optional station tool can be required; the adapter determines ownership and proficiency.
Stations.Point6: Public projects are always allowed. Personal inventory projects require at least one category selected in the station configuration.

Projects.Title: Projects and recipes
Projects.Text: Projects define the target, requirements, costs, and result of a downtime activity.
Projects.Point1: New projects copy the configured project base Item and are stored in the Downtime Projects Item folder.
Projects.Point2: Required total progress sets the target; Repeatable resets progress after successful completion.
Projects.Point3: Required Items are consumed once at project start. Required tools are checked but not consumed.
Projects.Point4: Completion costs are consumed only immediately before rewards are granted successfully.
Projects.Point5: At least one Item or character reward is required. An Item quantity of 0 lets the result table determine its quantity.
Projects.Point6: A project result table overrides the station table for that project.
Projects.Point7: Projects can be disabled or cancelled through the Downtime Overview or station; spent resources remain spent.
Projects.Point8: Character rewards can grant languages, proficiencies, and other character features supported by the active system adapter.
Projects.Point9: Collaborative projects have a project leader; other participants can join and contribute their own downtime to shared progress.

Templates.Title: Project and station presets
Templates.Text: Presets provide editable starting points, not immutable special rules.
Templates.Point1: Project templates can be created centrally in the Project Library or directly under a station's Public Projects.
Templates.Point2: Crafting, Research, Work, Training, Carousing, and Recovery provide suitable names, images, descriptions, and base values.
Templates.Point3: The project Item is created only on Save. Closing discards the draft; creation inside a station assigns the saved project automatically.
Templates.Point4: World-specific rewards, materials, costs, and tools must be added through drag and drop before saving.
Templates.Point5: Station presets create freely editable Actors in the Downtime Station Actor folder and can optionally create a matching project draft.
Templates.Point6: Work, research, training, and carousing station presets follow the intended downtime rules and remain fully editable after import.

Progress.Title: Progress calculation
Progress.Text: A station calculates progress from downtime, sources, modifiers, roll results, and an optional character value.
Progress.Point1: Base value applies per invested downtime and forms the starting point.
Progress.Point2: Character level, general proficiency bonus, and check proficiency can be enabled and multiplied separately.
Progress.Point3: Additional modifiers add to or multiply progress and do not alter rewards.
Progress.Point4: Result tables can add to or multiply progress and reward quantity separately.
Progress.Point5: An optional character value such as reputation can be stored for this station only or shared by a selected station category.
Progress.Point6: The formula preview shows the formula and included example values before use.

Checks.Title: Checks and result tables
Checks.Text: Checks come from the active system adapter and are only offered when supported by the game system.
Checks.Point1: Require a check requests one selected allowed check after each completed roll interval.
Checks.Point2: The table can evaluate either the natural d20 result or the full total.
Checks.Point3: Natural 1 and 20 have optional fixed rows; otherwise configured numeric ranges apply.
Checks.Point4: A pending check blocks further investment until it is rolled and resolved.
Checks.Point5: The selected check's proficiency or expertise can be used as a separate progress source.

Completion.Title: Completion checks
Completion.Text: A project can require a successful check against a DC after reaching its progress target.
Completion.Point1: Enable the completion check with its DC and retry cost in the project's Completion section.
Completion.Point2: After reaching the target, progress remains and the project waits for the check.
Completion.Point3: The first attempt is free; only attempts after a failure cost the configured downtime.
Completion.Point4: All failures behave equally: no rewards, no completion costs, and no progress loss.
Completion.Point5: Only success consumes completion costs, grants rewards, and completes or resets the project.

Dashboard.Title: Downtime overview
Dashboard.Text: The Downtime Overview is the central GM view for active projects and direct downtime.
Dashboard.Point1: Open the Downtime Manager control group at the bottom of the left canvas toolbar, directly below Journal Notes, then choose Downtime Overview. No token selection is required.
Dashboard.Point2: Project Overview shows character, station, project, progress, and status and can be collapsed.
Dashboard.Point3: The Project Library is directly available through the button at the top of the Downtime Overview.
Dashboard.Point4: The row delete button only cancels that character's progress; permanent project deletion happens in the library.
Dashboard.Point5: Under Direct Downtime, a GM can grant a flat amount to selected or all included PCs; the last global grant remains visible.
Dashboard.Point6: The separate Actor Folders for Player Selection module-setting entry controls which PCs appear; all subfolders are included automatically.

Sessions.Title: Session manager
Sessions.Text: The session manager grants configurable Item rewards, milestones, and downtime to selected characters.
Sessions.Point1: Open the Downtime Manager control group at the bottom of the left canvas toolbar, directly below Journal Notes, then choose Session Manager. No token selection is required; it can also be opened from the Downtime Overview.
Sessions.Point2: Players can be selected manually, from connected users, or from controlled tokens.
Sessions.Point3: Each reward column and milestone distribution can be toggled independently.
Sessions.Point4: Session rewards are droppable Items only and are configured by character level.
Sessions.Point5: With history disabled, title and description are optional and the session need not be saved separately before awarding.
Sessions.Point6: Awarding has a confirmation and locking state to prevent accidental parallel duplicate distribution.
Sessions.Point7: The separate Actor Folders for Player Selection module-setting entry limits the list to PCs in one selected root folder and all of its subfolders.

Passive.Title: Passive and direct downtime
Passive.Text: Passive downtime compensates for missed sessions; direct downtime is an immediate GM grant.
Passive.Point1: Passive downtime is configured in Session Rewards and can use weekly or monthly periods.
Passive.Point2: Rate and cap are based on configured downtime-Item rewards for the character's level.
Passive.Point3: Unselected characters accumulate passive downtime for a missed session up to the period cap.
Passive.Point4: Period settlement transfers accumulated passive downtime to the normal balance.
Passive.Point5: Direct downtime in the dashboard is a flat amount independent of level, session rewards, and percentages.

DowntimeItems.Title: Redeemable downtime Items
DowntimeItems.Text: An Item can be marked so that native use grants downtime to its owning character or displays the current balance.
DowntimeItems.Point1: Open the Item, choose Configure Downtime in its header, then Redeemable Downtime.
DowntimeItems.Point2: Set the granted amount; the module-managed description on the Item updates automatically.
DowntimeItems.Point3: In supported systems, native Item use is the main path and the system handles consumption.
DowntimeItems.Point4: Where no native hook exists, the module's fallback redemption can be used.
DowntimeItems.Point5: An Item can be either a downtime project or a redeemable downtime Item, not both at once.
DowntimeItems.Point6: An amount of 0 does not grant downtime; it displays the character's current downtime balance.

Settings.Title: Module settings
Settings.Text: World settings define default documents, categories, player selection, and administration features.
Settings.Point1: Downtime Manager Configuration manages the project base Item, default cost Item, and category catalog.
Settings.Point2: The Project Library is available here independently of a station and manages all world-level downtime projects.
Settings.Point3: The default cost Item can be added to project costs with one click; Black Flag defaults to its gold Item.
Settings.Point4: The category catalog defines the categories available to stations and projects.
Settings.Point5: Actor Folders for Player Selection is a separate entry alongside Session History and Station Presets; with no selection, all PCs appear.
Settings.Point6: A selected root folder limits session management and direct downtime and automatically includes every subfolder.
Settings.Point7: Project Library and Station Presets also have separate settings menus; world settings are GM-controlled, while this help page remains available to players.

Systems.Title: System adapters
Systems.Text: Adapters separate system-specific data and roll methods from general downtime logic.
Systems.Point1: Black Flag supports checks, level, proficiency values, Item quantities, prices, currency, character rewards, and native downtime Items.
Systems.Point2: Unknown systems use the generic adapter: projects and downtime work, but system-dependent checks, currency, and character rewards are not automatic.
Systems.Point3: Other modules can register an adapter through game.downtimeManager.registerSystemAdapter.
Systems.Point4: If expected checks or Item use are missing, first verify whether the active adapter supports that capability.

Troubleshooting.Title: Troubleshooting
Troubleshooting.Text: Most problems come from missing documents, permissions, or an older module build still being loaded.
Troubleshooting.Point1: If project creation fails, first configure a valid project base Item in Module Settings.
Troubleshooting.Point2: If a project cannot start, check rewards, materials, tools, Actor ownership, and available downtime.
Troubleshooting.Point3: If no check appears, verify the system adapter, Require a check, and allowed station checks.
Troubleshooting.Point4: If a reward is missing, verify that its referenced source Item still exists and is accessible.
Troubleshooting.Point5: Fully reload Foundry after module updates; an old version in the console indicates caching or an older loaded build.
Troubleshooting.Point6: The browser console shows technical errors. Record the error, action, Actor, station, and project for a reproducible report.
